title: Micranthes eriophora
intro: Micranthes eriophora, also known as the Appalachian wintergreen or American wintergreen, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to eastern North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Micranthes eriophora include methyl salicylate, salicylic acid, and other phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Micranthes eriophora include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate muscle and joint pain, as well as to support digestive health.
taxonomy: Micranthes eriophora is a species of flowering plant in the family Saxifragaceae. It is a perennial herb native to North America. The plant is classified within the genus Micranthes, which was previously divided into the genera Saxifraga and Mielichhoferia. The species is further categorized as being within the subfamily Saxifragoideae and the order Saxifragales. Its classification is essential for understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological roles.
